items.q: In Pavlov's experiment, what is the conditioned response?
items.correct: Salivation to the bell alone, after pairing with food.
items.misconception: Salivation to the food (which is automatic).
items.random_wrong: The dog ringing the bell itself.
items.why: Students confuse the unconditioned response (automatic salivation to food) with the conditioned response (learned salivation to bell). The conditioned response is the LEARNED one.

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.q: In Milgram's study, what percentage of participants gave the maximum 450V shock?
items.why: Students remember 'most' obeyed and assume all did. Actually 65% went to 450V; 35% defied. This nuance matters because it shows obedience is strong but not absolute.

items.q: In a reflex arc, the effector is usually a:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Muscle. “Receptor cell” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Tend-and-befriend was proposed as a response shown more in:
items.why: It's Females. “Children” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many neurons typically take part in a basic reflex arc?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
